questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The motion of an object along a straight line is one-dimensional.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Any motion can be resolved into a component along a straight line.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The motion of an object along a straight line is indeed one-dimensional. Any motion can be resolved into a component along a straight line, making this reason a correct explanation of the assertion.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): If the position of an object is given by s = 3t^2 + 2t + 1, then the object is moving uniformly.
questions.reason: Reason (R): For an object to be moving uniformly, its velocity should be constant.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The given equation of position is not linear, indicating that the velocity of the object is not constant, and hence, the object is not moving uniformly.

(D) A is false but R is true
